content: We can frame the development of a child (labeled here as child2, the second child in a family) as a biological, lived analog of the Reflexion autonomous AI agent, where the older sibling acts as the fixed baseline model and the second child iteratively improves their own social and problem-solving strategies via constant self-reflection on their older sibling’s missteps. This perspective leads to a testable hypothesis that second-born children will score higher on measures of adaptive self-reflection than firstborn children when controlling for family size and parenting style.
